trades.market: Two consecutive quarters of negative real GDP from Q4 2026 through Q4 2027
trades.catalyst: Upcoming BEA GDP estimates will clarify the growth trajectory; the Q2 2026 Advance Estimate on July 30 is the nearest major repricing event.
trades.risk_notes: Persistent inflation and restrictive monetary policy could produce a downturn, while the contract needs only one pair of consecutive negative quarters within its five-quarter window. Advance GDP estimates are also revision-prone.
trades.grounding_source: U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis, Gross Domestic Product releases: https://www.bea.gov/data/gdp/gross-domestic-product

avoid.market: Will the Fed cut rates 0 times in 2026?
avoid.reason: The 80.2% implied probability is broadly consistent with the inflationary signals in the supplied CPI and energy markets; no credible 8-point macro edge.

avoid.market: Will the Federal Reserve cut rates before 2027?
avoid.reason: The 22.65% implied probability is approximately consistent with the complementary zero-cut contract and elevated inflation expectations; insufficient edge.

avoid.market: WTI September 2026 settlement above $77.99 on July 17
avoid.reason: Same-day Brent thresholds broadly support a high WTI-above-threshold probability, but no live underlying futures reference was supplied to establish the required 10-point edge.
Excluded by the mandatory volume filter because total volume was below $50,000.

notes: The supplied market curve indicates an inflationary regime: July year-over-year CPI is concentrated near 3.3%–3.5%, crude oil thresholds imply elevated energy prices, and markets assign a low probability to a 2026 Fed cut. That combination argues against aggressive easing but does not by itself justify a 40.5% probability of two consecutive negative GDP quarters during the specified 2027 window.
